findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HTML sanitizer bypass via unsafe attribute values in LinkFilter
 * @description Check if LinkFilter allows dangerous href/src values like javascript: or data: URIs
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xss-link-filter-bypass
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  m = mc.getMethod() and
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LinkFilter") and
  m.hasName("filter")
select mc, "LinkFilter.filter() call - check for unsafe URL handling"
findings.hypothesis: HTML sanitizer LinkFilter may allow javascript: or data: URIs in href/src attributes

message.text: LinkFilter.filter() call - check for unsafe URL handling

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LinkFilter URL protocol validation - all string literals
 * @description Examine all string literals in LinkFilter to understand protocol validation
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/link-filter-strings
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

from Class c, StringLiteral sl
where
  c.hasName("LinkFilter") and
  sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType() = c
select sl, "LinkFilter literal: '" + sl.getValue() + "' at line " + sl.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Examine all string literals in LinkFilter to understand how URL protocols are validated

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HTMLDiffMarker - HTML string construction with user data
 * @description Check if HTML diff output is constructed with unescaped data 
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/html-diff-xss
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

// Look for string concatenation in diff-related HTML generation classes
from Class c, AddExpr ae, Method m
where
  (c.hasName("HTMLDiffMarker") or c.hasName("AbstractXMLDiffMarker") or c.hasName("UnifiedHTMLDiffManager")) and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  ae.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    ae.getLeftOperand().getType() instanceof TypeString or
    ae.getRightOperand().getType() instanceof TypeString
  )
select ae, c.getName() + "." + m.getName() + "() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line " + ae.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: HTML diff generation may concatenate unescaped user data into HTML output

AbstractXMLDiffMarker.removeTextWrappers() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 482
AbstractXMLDiffMarker.removeTextWrappers() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 482
AbstractXMLDiffMarker.removeTextWrappers() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 482
HTMLDiffMarker.insertRightBlocks() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 252
HTMLDiffMarker.insertRightBlocks() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 252
HTMLDiffMarker.insertRightBlocks() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 252
HTMLDiffMarker.insertRightBlocks() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 252
UnifiedHTMLDiffManager.wrap() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 132
UnifiedHTMLDiffManager.wrap() - string concatenation that may build HTML at line 132

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name UnifiedHTMLDiffManager wrap() and HTMLDiffMarker - detailed string concat analysis
 * @description Check if the HTML diff classes construct HTML with potentially unescaped user content
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/html-diff-detailed
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

// Find string concat operations where one side is an HTML tag literal and the other might be user data
from Class c, Method m, AddExpr ae, StringLiteral sl
where
  (c.hasName("HTMLDiffMarker") or c.hasName("AbstractXMLDiffMarker") or c.hasName("UnifiedHTMLDiffManager")) and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  ae.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  sl.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    sl.getValue().matches("<%") or
    sl.getValue().matches("%>%") or
    sl.getValue().matches("%<%") or
    sl.getValue().matches("<%/%>") or
    sl.getValue() = "<" or
    sl.getValue() = ">"
  )
select ae, c.getName() + "." + m.getName() + "() at line " + ae.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - building HTML with concat near tag literal: '" + sl.getValue() + "'"
findings.hypothesis: HTML diff classes build HTML with string concatenation that may include unescaped user data

UnifiedHTMLDiffManager.wrap() at line 132 - building HTML with concat near tag literal: '</body></html>'

UnifiedHTMLDiffManager.wrap() at line 132 - building HTML with concat near tag literal: '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><!DOCTYPE html><html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"><head></head><body>'
UnifiedHTMLDiffManager.wrap() at line 132 - building HTML with concat near tag literal: '</body></html>'

findings.hypothesis: Jackson ObjectMapper may not be configured to HTML-escape characters like < > & / in JSON output, making it unsafe for embedding in HTML pages (XSS via JSONP or inline scripts)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Jackson ObjectMapper HTML escaping configuration check
 * @description Check if ObjectMapper is created with factory that disables HTML escaping
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/jackson-disable-html-escape
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */
import java

// Check for JsonFactory or ObjectMapper that calls disable with ESCAPE_NON_ASCII 
// or where factory character escaping is disabled for HTML-unsafe chars
from ClassInstanceExpr cie, Method m
where
  (
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.fasterxml.jackson.databind", "ObjectMapper") or
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.fasterxml.jackson.core", "JsonFactory")
  ) and
  m = cie.getEnclosingCallable()
select cie, "New ObjectMapper/JsonFactory created in " + m.getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + m.getName() + "() at line " + cie.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - check if HTML escaping is configured"
findings.hypothesis: Jackson ObjectMapper instances may be created without HTML-safe character escaping

message.text: New ObjectMapper/JsonFactory created in JsonConverter.<obinit>() at line 41 - check if HTML escaping is configured
New ObjectMapper/JsonFactory created in JSONTool.serialize() at line 71 - check if HTML escaping is configured
New ObjectMapper/JsonFactory created in JSONTool.fromString() at line 95 - check if HTML escaping is configured

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name JSONTool serialize without HTML escaping - XSS risk
 * @description JSONTool.serialize() creates ObjectMapper without configuring HTML-safe escaping.
 * By default Jackson does not escape HTML special chars like < > & in JSON output.
 * When Velocity templates embed $jsontool.serialize(obj) directly in HTML, this can lead to XSS.
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/jsontool-html-unsafe-confirmed
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */
import java

// Find the serialize method and check there's no HTML-safe factory configuration
from Method m, ClassInstanceExpr mapperCreation
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("JSONTool") and
  m.hasName("serialize") and
  mapperCreation.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  mapperCreation.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.fasterxml.jackson.databind", "ObjectMapper") and
  // Confirm there's no call to configure HTML-safe factory
  not exists(MethodCall mc |
    mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
    (
      mc.getMethod().hasName("setCharacterEscapes") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("quoteAsText") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("disable") and mc.getArgument(0).toString().matches("%ESCAPE_NON_ASCII%")
    )
  )
select mapperCreation, "JSONTool.serialize() creates ObjectMapper at line " + mapperCreation.getLocation().getStartLine() + " without HTML-safe character escaping. JSON output containing '<', '>', '&', '\"' will not be escaped, allowing XSS when output is embedded in HTML pages."
findings.hypothesis: JSONTool.serialize() uses Jackson ObjectMapper without HTML-safe escaping - JSON with unescaped < > & characters can cause XSS when embedded in HTML

message.text: JSONTool.serialize() creates ObjectMapper at line 71 without HTML-safe character escaping. JSON output containing '<', '>', '&', '"' will not be escaped, allowing XSS when output is embedded in HTML pages.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name AttributeFilter - dangerous HTML attributes allowed
 * @description Check if AttributeFilter properly blocks event handler attributes and dangerous attributes

 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

from Class c, StringLiteral sl

select sl, "AttributeFilter literal: '" + sl.getValue() + "' at line " + sl.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: AttributeFilter may not properly block event handler attributes (onclick, onload, etc.) enabling XSS

message.text: AttributeFilter literal: 'Failed to apply the HTML attribute cleaning filter.' at line 100

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name JSONTool serialize - Jackson ObjectMapper without ESCAPE_NON_ASCII configuration
 * @description Confirms JSONTool.serialize() uses plain ObjectMapper without HTML-safe escaping,
 * making it unsafe when output is embedded in HTML. Jackson by default does not escape
 * < > & / ' characters unless configured with a character escapes factory.
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/jsontool-no-html-escape-jackson
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */
import java

// Find the exact configure calls near ObjectMapper creation in JSONTool
from Method serialize, ClassInstanceExpr mapperCreation, MethodCall writeCall
where
  serialize.getDeclaringType().hasName("JSONTool") and
  serialize.hasName("serialize") and
  mapperCreation.getEnclosingCallable() = serialize and
  mapperCreation.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.fasterxml.jackson.databind", "ObjectMapper") and
  writeCall.getEnclosingCallable() = serialize and
  writeCall.getMethod().hasName("writeValueAsString") and
  // verify no HTML-safe configuration
  not exists(MethodCall safeConfig |
    safeConfig.getEnclosingCallable() = serialize and
    (
      safeConfig.getMethod().hasName("setCharacterEscapes") or
      safeConfig.getMethod().hasName("configure") and safeConfig.getAnArgument().toString().matches("%ESCAPE%")
    )
  )
select writeCall, "JSONTool.serialize() calls Jackson ObjectMapper.writeValueAsString() without HTML-safe escaping. Characters '<', '>', '&', '\"' in serialized JSON are not escaped, enabling XSS injection when the JSON output is embedded in HTML pages or used in Velocity templates rendered as HTML."
findings.hypothesis: JSONTool.serialize() produces HTML-unsafe JSON. Jackson's default ObjectMapper does not escape HTML special characters, so strings like </script><script>alert(1)</script> in serialized data can execute JavaScript.

message.text: JSONTool.serialize() calls Jackson ObjectMapper.writeValueAsString() without HTML-safe escaping. Characters '<', '>', '&', '"' in serialized JSON are not escaped, enabling XSS injection when the JSON output is embedded in HTML pages or used in Velocity templates rendered as HTML.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name InsecureHTMLElementSanitizer - allows all HTML including scripts
 * @description InsecureHTMLElementSanitizer bypasses security by allowing all HTML content
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/insecure-html-sanitizer
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

// Check InsecureHTMLElementSanitizer implementation - what it returns for isElementAllowed/isAttributeAllowed
from Class c, Method m, ReturnStmt ret
where
  c.hasName("InsecureHTMLElementSanitizer") and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  ret.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select ret, "InsecureHTMLElementSanitizer." + m.getName() + "() returns: " + ret.getResult().toString() + " at line " + ret.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: InsecureHTMLElementSanitizer may bypass all security checks by returning true for all elements/attributes

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name EscapeTool and JSONTool - potential XSS bypass in Velocity tools
 * @description Check EscapeTool and JSONTool for methods that might not properly escape HTML/JS
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/velocity-tools-xss
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */
import java

// Check JSONTool for HTML-unsafe JSON serialization
from Class c, Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  (c.hasName("JSONTool") or c.hasName("EscapeTool")) and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("serialize") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toJSON") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toJSONString") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("escape") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("xml") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("html")
  )
select mc, c.getName() + "." + m.getName() + "() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Velocity EscapeTool or JSONTool may not properly escape for HTML/JS contexts enabling XSS

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name NetfluxEndpoint WebSocket message echo without encoding
 * @description Check if WebSocket messages are echoed back to clients without HTML encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/websocket-xss-netflux
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

// Find where messages are sent back in NetfluxEndpoint
from Class c, Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  c.hasName("NetfluxEndpoint") and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("sendText") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("sendString") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("sendObject") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("sendBinary") or
    (mc.getMethod().hasName("send") and mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("javax.websocket", "RemoteEndpoint"))
  )
select mc, "NetfluxEndpoint." + m.getName() + "() sends message at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: NetfluxEndpoint WebSocket handler may echo user messages without encoding

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name JSONTool serialize method - HTML-unsafe JSON output
 * @description Check if JSONTool.serialize() produces HTML-unsafe output (< > & not escaped)
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/jsontool-html-unsafe
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */
import java

// Find the serialize method in JSONTool and how it calls ObjectMapper/Jackson
from Class c, Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  c.hasName("JSONTool") and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  (m.hasName("serialize") or m.hasName("toJSON")) and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select mc, "JSONTool." + m.getName() + "() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() on " + mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: JSONTool.serialize() may produce HTML-unsafe JSON allowing XSS when embedded in HTML pages

JSONTool.serialize() calls addSerializer() on SimpleModule at line 74

JSONTool.serialize() calls writeValueAsString() on ObjectMapper at line 77
JSONTool.serialize() calls error() on Logger at line 79

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name DiffHTMLScriptService and DiffDisplayerScriptService - XSS via unescaped diff output
 * @description Check diff script services that produce HTML output from user-controlled content
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/diff-script-service-xss
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

// Check methods in diff script services that return HTML content
from Class c, Method m
where
  (c.hasName("DiffHTMLScriptService") or c.hasName("DiffDisplayerScriptService") or c.hasName("DiffScriptService")) and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c and
  not m.isPrivate() and
  not m.getName() = "<init>"
select m, c.getName() + "." + m.getName() + "() returns " + m.getReturnType().toString() + " at line " + m.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Diff script services return HTML from user-controlled content without proper escaping

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() returns raw HTML String from user content
 * @description DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() takes two HTML strings (possibly user-controlled)
 * and returns a raw HTML diff string. If the input HTML is not properly sanitized, this could
 * allow stored/reflected XSS through the diff view.
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/diff-html-raw-string-output
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */
import java

// Check DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() method - what parameters it takes and what it returns
from Method m, Parameter p
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("DiffHTMLScriptService") and
  m.hasName("unified") and
  p.getCallable() = m
select p, "DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() parameter: " + p.getName() + " of type " + p.getType().toString() + " - returns raw HTML String from potentially user-controlled content"
findings.hypothesis: DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() returns raw HTML String from user-controlled HTML inputs, potentially allowing XSS through diff view

message.text: DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() parameter: previousHTML of type String - returns raw HTML String from potentially user-controlled content
DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() parameter: nextHTML of type String - returns raw HTML String from potentially user-controlled content
DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() parameter: config of type XMLDiffConfiguration - returns raw HTML String from potentially user-controlled content
DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() parameter: previousHTML of type String - returns raw HTML String from potentially user-controlled content
DiffHTMLScriptService.unified() parameter: nextHTML of type String - returns raw HTML String from potentially user-controlled content

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name DefaultHTMLElementSanitizer - bypass via configuration
 * @description Check how DefaultHTMLElementSanitizer can be bypassed or misconfigured
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/default-html-element-sanitizer-bypass
 * @tags security

 */
import java

// Check DefaultHTMLElementSanitizer - how it decides if elements/attributes are allowed
